title: About the Project
tagline: Real info for people who actually keep fish
story.p1: I built Aqua360 because I was tired of seeing people lose fish over bad info. Who hasn't seen someone toss Neon Tetras in with Angelfish and then wonder why the Neons started disappearing? Or mix species with completely different water parameters and just hope for the best?
story.p2: The idea is simple: before you set up your tank, you can check here whether the fish, plants, and invertebrates you want actually work together. Predation risk, behavior, pH, temperature -- it's all here, with data on over 788 cataloged species.
story.p3: No ads, no affiliate links, no one trying to sell you anything. Just a tool made by a dev who's also into fishkeeping and wanted something like this for himself. If it helps you out, that's a win.
feature.compatibility.desc: Check if your species can actually live together without stress, predation, or fighting.
feature.catalog.desc: 707 freshwater fish, saltwater fish, and invertebrates with full profiles.
feature.plants.desc: 148 species with lighting, CO2, difficulty, and placement info.
feature.corals.desc: 37 marine species with photos, parameters, and difficulty level.
feature.calculators.desc: Volume, filtration, substrate, heater sizing -- 10 useful tools.
feature.guides.desc: How to set up community, planted, cichlid, and marine tanks from scratch.
principle.noAds.desc: No banners, no pop-ups, nothing getting in your way. Just content.
principle.transparent.desc: Nobody's trying to sell you anything here. The info comes from scientific data, not commercial partnerships.
principle.community.desc: The project is free and independent. I maintain it in my spare time because I believe good info should be accessible.
tech.desc: Built with React, TypeScript, and Tailwind. Scientific data comes from GBIF, WoRMS, and iNaturalist.
